title: REIMS
text: A direct-to-analysis technique that allows near-instantaneous chemical analysis of a sample with minimal to no preparation. A heated blade vaporizes tissue, and the resulting aerosol is directed into a mass spectrometer, producing a chemical fingerprint in seconds.
meta: Rapid Evaporative Ionization Mass Spectrometry

title: m/z
text: The x-axis of a mass spectrum, representing the ratio of an ion's mass to its charge. The REIMS dataset spans 2,080 distinct m/z features from approximately 77.04 to 999.32 m/z, each corresponding to a different molecular ion.
meta: Mass-to-Charge Ratio

title: TIC
text: The sum of all detected ion intensities across all m/z values in a single mass spectrum. TIC normalization divides each feature by the total ion current to remove inter-sample variation in overall signal intensity.
meta: Total Ion Current

title: OPLS-DA
text: A supervised chemometrics classification method that separates variation correlated with class labels (predictive) from orthogonal variation. Used as the primary baseline in this thesis, achieving up to 96% accuracy on species identification.
meta: Orthogonal Partial Least Squares Discriminant Analysis

title: Batch Detection
text: The task of determining whether two fish samples originate from the same processing batch. Enables rapid recalls if contamination is discovered. Formulated as a pairwise classification task using contrastive learning in this thesis.
meta: Batch Detection

title: Cross-species Adulteration
text: Food fraud where a high-value fish species (Hoki) is mixed with a cheaper species (Mackerel). Formulated as a 3-class problem: pure Hoki, pure Mackerel, or a 50/50 mixture. Deep learning achieves 91.97% accuracy vs. 79.96% for OPLS-DA.
meta: Cross-species Adulteration

title: Oil Contamination
text: Detection and quantification of oil (e.g., engine or processing equipment oil) introduced into fish samples. Formulated as a 7-class ordinal classification problem with oil concentrations from 0% to 50% in 10% increments.
meta: Oil Contamination Detection

title: Species Identification
text: Classifying a REIMS spectrum to determine the fish species. A 2-class problem (Hoki vs. Mackerel) in this thesis, where MoE Transformer achieves 100% accuracy vs. 96.39% for OPLS-DA.
meta: Fish Species Identification

title: Body Part Identification
text: Classifying which anatomical body part a fish sample originates from (e.g., fillet, frame, offal). A multi-class problem where Ensemble Transformer achieves 74.13% accuracy vs. 51.17% for OPLS-DA.
meta: Fish Body Part Identification

title: Marine Biomass
text: The total mass of all living marine organisms within a given area or ecosystem. In the context of this thesis, refers to the biological material (fish and shellfish) analyzed using REIMS for food quality and traceability applications.
meta: Marine Biomass

title: Transformer
text: A deep learning architecture based on self-attention mechanisms, introduced by Vaswani et al. (2017). Processes all elements of a sequence in parallel, capturing long-range dependencies. Applied to REIMS spectra as sequences of m/z values in this thesis.
meta: Transformer Neural Network

title: MoE
text: A neural network architecture where multiple specialized sub-networks (experts) process different inputs, gated by a learned router. The MoE Transformer achieves 100% species identification accuracy by routing different spectral regions to specialized expert networks.
meta: Mixture of Experts

title: SpectroSim
text: A novel contrastive learning framework introduced in this thesis for label-free batch traceability. Uses a Transformer encoder within the SimCLR framework to learn pairwise similarity between mass spectra, achieving 70.8% batch detection accuracy without labeled data.
meta: SpectroSim

title: MSM
text: A novel self-supervised pre-training technique introduced in this thesis, adapting BERT's masked language modeling to sequential REIMS data. Random m/z features are masked and the model learns to reconstruct them, providing a useful initialization for downstream tasks.
meta: Masked Spectra Modelling

title: Gone Phishing
text: A novel Mixture of Experts (MoE) Transformer architecture introduced in this thesis for REIMS-based fish fraud detection — the name is a pun on fish and phishing. It replaces the standard feed-forward networks inside each Transformer encoder block with MoE layers: a learned gating mechanism routes each input token to the Top-k most relevant expert sub-networks, whose outputs are combined by a weighted sum. This allows the model to scale capacity without proportionally increasing compute. Achieves 100% accuracy on fish species identification.
meta: Gone Phishing (MoE Transformer)

title: Autobots
text: A stacked voting ensemble of multi-scale Transformers introduced in this thesis — the name is a pun on the Autobots, a team of diverse Transformers. Three independent Transformer models with 2, 4, and 8 layers/heads respectively act as level-0 base classifiers analyzing the spectrum at low, medium, and high resolution. Their outputs are fed into a learned weighted combination meta-model (level-1) that optimally combines each model's predictions. Achieves 74.13% accuracy on fish body part identification.
meta: Autobots (Multi-scale Ensemble Transformer)

title: Ensemble Transformer
text: An architecture combining multiple Transformer models trained with different initializations or configurations. Achieves 74.13% body part identification accuracy by aggregating predictions from multiple specialized models.
meta: Ensemble Transformer

title: LIME
text: An explainability technique that approximates any black-box model locally with an interpretable surrogate. Applied to REIMS models in this thesis to identify which m/z features most influence individual predictions.
meta: Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations

title: Grad-CAM
text: An explainability technique that uses gradients flowing into the final convolutional or attention layer to produce a saliency map. Applied to Transformer models in this thesis to visualize spectral regions important for classification.
meta: Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping

title: Transfer Learning
text: A machine learning approach where a model pre-trained on one task or dataset is fine-tuned on a different but related task. In this thesis, models trained on species identification are adapted to oil contamination detection, improving accuracy by up to 22.67%.
meta: Transfer Learning

title: Contrastive Learning
text: A self-supervised learning paradigm that trains models to pull representations of similar samples together and push dissimilar samples apart. Used in SpectroSim for batch traceability without requiring labeled batch data.
meta: Contrastive Learning

title: SimCLR
text: A contrastive learning framework by Chen et al. (2020) that learns representations by maximizing agreement between differently augmented views of the same sample. SpectroSim adapts this framework for mass spectra.
meta: Simple Contrastive Learning of Representations

title: BERT
text: A pre-training language model using masked language modeling. Its masked-token objective inspired the Masked Spectra Modelling (MSM) technique in this thesis.
meta: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers

title: Self-supervised Learning
text: A machine learning paradigm that generates supervisory signals from the data itself, without human-labeled annotations. Used in this thesis for MSM pre-training and SpectroSim contrastive learning on REIMS spectra.
meta: Self-supervised Learning

title: Hoki
text: A deep-sea fish species native to New Zealand waters, used as the high-value species in cross-species adulteration experiments. New Zealand is the world's largest exporter of Hoki, making it a target for seafood fraud.
meta: Hoki (Macruronus novaezelandiae)

title: Mackerel
text: A pelagic fish species used as the adulterant in cross-species adulteration experiments. Less expensive than Hoki but with a similar appearance when processed, making it a common seafood fraud target.
meta: Mackerel (Scomber japonicus)

title: Food Fraud
text: Deliberate adulteration, mislabeling, or misrepresentation of food products for economic gain. Seafood fraud is estimated to affect 30% of commercially sold seafood globally, driving the need for rapid verification tools like REIMS.
meta: Food Fraud

title: Species Substitution
text: A form of food fraud where a premium fish species is replaced with a cheaper alternative. Detected in this thesis using REIMS-based classification, achieving up to 100% accuracy with Transformer models.
meta: Species Substitution

title: IUU Fishing
text: Fishing activities that contravene national and international laws, including fishing without authorization, under-reporting catches, and operating in restricted areas. REIMS-based species identification can help verify the provenance of seafood products.
meta: Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated Fishing

title: Chemical Fingerprint
text: The characteristic pattern of molecular ions detected in a mass spectrum, unique to a given biological sample. REIMS produces chemical fingerprints of tissue that encode species, body part, and contamination information.
meta: Chemical Fingerprint

title: Ordinal Classification
text: Classification where the target classes have a natural ordering (e.g., 0%, 10%, 20% oil concentration). Standard classification ignores this ordering; ordinal methods exploit it. Used for oil contamination detection in this thesis.
meta: Ordinal Classification

title: BCA
text: An accuracy metric that averages per-class recall, compensating for class imbalance. Used as the primary evaluation metric in this thesis to fairly compare models across unbalanced datasets.
meta: Balanced Classification Accuracy

title: MAE
text: The average absolute difference between predicted and true values. Used alongside BCA for ordinal classification tasks (oil contamination, adulteration) where the magnitude of classification error matters.
meta: Mean Absolute Error

title: Negative Ionization Mode
text: A mass spectrometry acquisition mode in which negatively charged ions are detected. REIMS in negative ionization mode primarily detects lipid-related compounds (fatty acids, phospholipids) that form the chemical fingerprint of fish tissue.
meta: Negative Ionization Mode

title: PCA
text: A linear dimensionality reduction technique that projects data onto axes of maximum variance. Used for visualization and as a preprocessing step, but outperformed by deep learning methods on REIMS classification tasks.
meta: Principal Component Analysis

title: SVM
text: A supervised learning algorithm that finds the optimal hyperplane separating classes in a high-dimensional feature space. Used as a baseline classifier in this thesis.
meta: Support Vector Machine

title: KNN
text: A non-parametric classification algorithm that assigns a label based on the majority class among the k nearest training samples. Used as a baseline classifier in this thesis.
meta: K-Nearest Neighbours
